title: Auto Paopulate PDF into form column When new item is created
excerpt:

posts.author: Willie (Bill)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday Community,

Like a lot of us, I am new to Monday.com but I am enjoying exploring it countless features. One thing that I’ve been searching for but haven’t come across yet Is the ability to auto-populate a file column with a PDF when a new Item (job) is created.

I have a PDF form that we want to automatically populate our File field whenever a new Job is created.

Is this possible?

I apologize if this has been discussed before, but I can’t find that feed.

Kind Regards

Bill Hunter

posts.author: RaymondChin (Raymond Chin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Luckily, there is such a method!

Monday has a feature that lets you set default values for all sorts of columns;
This includes PDFs! Just look for “Item default Values” from the board menu as shown below.

image1082×277 22.5 KB




In the popup that follows, you can click on the file column you want to set a default file for and add it to the board.

From then on, whenever you create a new item in that board, it will come with a fresh copy of the file you uploaded.

posts.author: Willie (Bill)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Raymond, that’s exactly what I was looking for!!!

posts.author: Willie (Bill)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi again Raymond, thanks again for your reply.

With this now created, it turned my fillable form into a non-fillable form? Are you aware of anyway to amend this?

posts.author: RaymondChin (Raymond Chin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bill,

Unfortunately not. I haven’t really had the chance to experiment with editable pdfs in Monday, so I’m not entirely sure what the root of your issue is.
